title: Fix dashboard timeout

tasks.title: Diagnose and fix live dashboard `/api/state` timeout
tasks.verification: RED: bun test bin/lib/dashboard/server.test.ts failed because DEFAULT_DASHBOARD_READER_TIMEOUT_MS was missing. GREEN: server.test.ts passed (12 tests) after exporting 5s default. VERIFY: bun test bin/lib/dashboard bin/lib/live-status.test.ts passed (69 tests).

proposal.approval_evidence: User explicitly requested `fix issues, give me live url` after live `/api/state` timeout was discovered. Scope: restore live dashboard data API health while preserving local/read-only/worker-free behavior.

discovery.approval_evidence: Contract minted (9 items). Root cause confirmed: live health checker used an 8s request budget, while dashboard default source-reader timeout was 10s. Slow source reads could outlive caller budget. Fix direction: default interactive reader budget <=5s and degrade slow source(s).

design.approval_evidence: Design implemented: export DEFAULT_DASHBOARD_READER_TIMEOUT_MS=5000 and use it as buildDashboardState default, preserving caller override and existing per-source withTimeout degradation. No auth/read model/mutation changes.

planning.approval_evidence: User requested immediate fix/live URL. One task created and completed: diagnose/fix live dashboard /api/state timeout with TDD evidence.

execution.approval_evidence: Task completed and checkpointed at 4d6a787b5b5cd1d7d00cc381acd7080b864971e4. RED: server test failed on missing timeout constant. GREEN: server test passed (12). VERIFY: dashboard/live-status suite passed (69). Trunk fast-forwarded to checkpoint for live service.

acceptance.approval_evidence: User requested `fix issues, give me live url`; delivered and live-verified. / returned 200; /api/state returned 200 bytes=69383 within 8s; /api/change/pokeedge/fixDeployDependency returned 200. Contract matrix 9/9 passing/respected.

documents.proposal:
# Fix dashboard timeout

## Problem

After `hardenDashboardReads` shipped, the live local dashboard service restarted and served `/` successfully, but `/api/state` timed out after 8 seconds. This means the shipped UI is loaded, but the data API is not live/healthy.

## Scope

- Diagnose the `/api/state` stall in the live dashboard service.
- Make `/api/state` bounded and responsive for the configured dashboard projects.
- Preserve local-only, read-only behavior.
- Preserve the worker-free routine ADV read intent from `rq-dashboardWorkerFree01`.
- Return the live dashboard URL after verification.

## Acceptance

- `/` returns HTTP 200.
- `/api/state` returns HTTP 200 within 8 seconds.
- At least one `/api/change/{projectId}/{changeId}` detail probe returns HTTP 200 when a visible change exists.
- Targeted tests cover the timeout fix.
- No mutation controls or ADV state-file links are introduced.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement

## Objectives

1. Restore live dashboard data API health.
2. Keep dashboard reads bounded, local-only, and read-only.
3. Preserve worker-free routine ADV summary behavior.
4. Provide the live URL once verified.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Given the dashboard service is running, when `GET /` is requested, then it returns HTTP 200.
2. Given the dashboard service is running, when `GET /api/state` is requested, then it returns HTTP 200 within 8 seconds.
3. Given `/api/state` returns at least one visible change, when `GET /api/change/{projectId}/{changeId}` is requested for one visible change, then it returns HTTP 200 within 8 seconds.
4. Given targeted dashboard tests run, when they complete, then the timeout regression is covered and tests pass.
5. Given the fix is applied, when source reads are slow/unavailable, then affected source(s) degrade rather than blocking the whole `/api/state` response.

## Constraints

- Local-only dashboard.
- Read-only; no mutation controls.
- No secrets or raw ADV state-file links.
- Do not reintroduce routine per-change workflow query fan-out.

## Sign-Off

User explicitly requested: `fix issues, give me live url`.
documents.design:
# Design

Use direct diagnosis first. The likely failure is a source reader not honoring the dashboard timeout boundary, causing `buildDashboardState` or a reader call to hang beyond the HTTP request budget. Keep the fix structural: ensure each configured source read is bounded and converted to degraded source output rather than allowing `/api/state` to stall.

Implementation will inspect the current dashboard state builder and source readers, add a failing regression if an existing path is unbounded, then fix the bounded/degraded behavior. Verification includes tests plus live service restart and HTTP checks.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary

Fixed the live local dashboard `/api/state` timeout discovered after `hardenDashboardReads` shipped. The dashboard default source-reader timeout was 10 seconds, while the live health check and user-visible expectation used an 8-second budget. Slow source reads could therefore make `/api/state` appear hung.

Implemented fix:
- Added `DEFAULT_DASHBOARD_READER_TIMEOUT_MS = 5_000`.
- Changed `buildDashboardState` to use the 5s default while preserving explicit overrides.
- Added a regression test that the default timeout fits the interactive `/api/state` health budget.

Verification:
- `bun test bin/lib/dashboard/server.test.ts` — 12 pass.
- `bun test bin/lib/dashboard bin/lib/live-status.test.ts` — 69 pass.
- Fast-forwarded trunk to commit `4d6a787b5b5cd1d7d00cc381acd7080b864971e4`.
- Restarted `adv-dashboard-pokeedge.service`.
- Live checks passed: `/` 200, `/api/state` 200 within 8s, `/api/change/pokeedge/fixDeployDependency` 200.

Live URL: http://127.0.0.1:8765/
